mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and wealth effects, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output, which provides immediate positive earnings momentum and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days exclusively during stable inflation periods.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 when CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer price inflation combined with a low federal funds rate creates negative real yields, prompting markets to price in aggressive future monetary tightening, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (t-stat < 1.5) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic capacity and consumer spending power, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll revisions consistently coincide with negative 60-day forward returns, yielding a win rate below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth falls below 1.0%, it signals severe economic contraction and deteriorating corporate profit margins, triggering broad equity sell-offs that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth is below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The concurrent rise in industrial production and decline in the unemployment rate confirms a synchronized economic upswing, boosting short-term market sentiment and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 5-day returns in >60% of occurrences during recovery phases.
